comment: Single source of truth for CI targets. resolve-targets.sh reads this to compute the default set, the enabled_targets CSV, and target_map (id -> entry) consumed by every standalone job in make.yml. kind (native | qemu | vm) is descriptive metadata for the kind of runner/emulation; it no longer drives a dynamic matrix. runner is the GitHub-hosted runner; for qemu/vm it is the host that runs the emulator/VM. fpc_target is the FPC platform triple. fpc_tarball_target is an OPTIONAL per-target override of the tarball filename token only (e.g. x86_64-freebsd11 for fpc-3.2.2.x86_64-freebsd11.tar); the download directory and extracted dir stay canonical (fpc_target). resolve-targets.sh defaults it to fpc_target when absent, so every target_map entry carries a concrete value; install-fpc-lazarus.sh also falls back to FPC_TARGET if the env is empty/unset. Every job gates via a job-level `if: contains(... enabled_targets ...)` and reads runner/fpc_target via target_map, but keeps a literal name: value (an if-skipped job renders an unevaluated name expression in the UI, so name cannot be sourced from here). default=true targets run automatically on push/PR; default=false targets are opt-in and must be named explicitly via workflow_dispatch enabled_targets (set default=false to stop a slow target from running automatically).
